import pytest
from source import find_in_sorted

def test_find_last_element_in_sorted_array():
    arr = [3, 4, 5, 6, 7]
    target = 7
    result = find_in_sorted(arr, target)
    assert result == 4, f"Expected index 4 for target {target} in array {arr}, but got {result}"

analysis.bug_hypothesis: When recursing into the upper half (x > arr[mid]), the function passes 'mid' as the new start instead of 'mid + 1', causing the search interval to include the already-checked mid element, leading to infinite recursion when the target is in the upper half
analysis.bug_location: binsearch function, line where x > arr[mid] condition is handled
analysis.trigger_condition: Target element is in the upper half of the array and specifically when the search narrows down to a two-element range where the target is the second element
analysis.expected_vs_actual: Expected: binsearch(mid + 1, end) to exclude the already-checked mid element. Actual: binsearch(mid, end) includes the mid element again, causing infinite recursion
analysis.suggested_test_strategy: Test with target as last element (find_in_sorted([3, 4, 5, 6, 7], 7)), target in upper half but not last (find_in_sorted([1, 2, 3, 4, 5], 4)), and edge case with two elements where target is second (find_in_sorted([6, 7], 7))
